data.title: Vår Energi Prices Offering of $1.50 Billion Senior Notes
data.description:
Vår Energi said Thursday it priced an offering of two series of senior notes with a combined principal amount of $1.50 billion.

The offering comprised $750 million of 5.875% senior notes due May 22, 2030, and $750 million of 6.500% senior notes due May 22, 3035. The 2030 notes are issued at a price equal to 99.812% of the principal amount, while the 2035 notes are issued at 99.406% of the principal amount.

Settlement of the notes is expected on May 22, with proceeds earmarked for the repayment of the Norwegian oil and gas company's credit facilities and general corporate expenditures.

data.title: Talanx 1Q Net Pft EUR604M
RBA's Narrow Policy Path May Be Widening at Last — Market Talk
data.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia has spoken in recent years about the narrow policy path before it as it seeks to balance its dual mandate of achieving full employment and inflation. It may be that the policy path is finally widening a little as job growth booms and inflation comes under control, says Su-Lin Ong, chief economist at RBC Capital. The jobs market and inflation data since the RBA board's last meeting supports some easing in the restrictive policy stance with a 25 basis point cut in the official cash rate on Tuesday to 3.85% looking likely, she adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: First Sponsor Group Issues SG$128 Million Worth of Bonds Due 2030
data.description:

The bonds, which were issued under the company's SG$1 billion multicurrency debt issuance program, were admitted to the Official List of the Singapore Exchange today, May 15.

data.title: Relief Therapeutics Publishes 2025 Annual General Meeting Agenda
data.description:
GENEVA, SWITZERLAND / ACCESS Newswire / May 15, 2025 / RELIEF THERAPEUTICS Holding SA (SIX:RLF)(OTCQB:RLFTF)(OTCQB:RLFTY ) (Relief), a biopharmaceutical company committed to delivering innovative treatment options for select specialty, unmet and rare diseases, today published the agenda for its 2025 Annual General Meeting (AGM) of shareholders, to be held on June 12, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. CEST in Geneva, Switzerland.

The agenda includes approval of the 2024 annual report and financial statements, appropriation of available earnings, discharge of the board of directors and executive committee, re-election of the members of the board of directors and its chairman, re-election of the members of the nomination and compensation committee, and approval of the maximum compensation amounts for the board of directors and executive committee. Also included are a consultative vote on the 2024 compensation report and the re-election of the independent voting rights representative and auditors.

The formal notice of convocation of the AGM, including information regarding attendance in person or by proxy, notification and registration requirements, and ancillary documents relevant to the meeting, will be mailed to registered shareholders on May 21, 2025. The full agenda is available on the Relief website .

ABOUT RELIEF

Relief is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to advancing treatment paradigms and improving the lives of patients with rare and debilitating diseases. With core expertise in drug delivery systems and drug repurposing, Relief's clinical pipeline includes innovative treatments designed to address critical unmet medical needs in rare dermatological, metabolic and respiratory conditions. The Company has also successfully brought several approved products to market through licensing and distribution partnerships. Headquartered in Geneva, Relief is listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the symbol RLF and quoted in the U.S. on OTCQB under the symbols RLFTF and RLFTY. For more information, visit www.relieftherapeutics.com

CONTACT

RELIEF THERAPEUTICS Holding SA

Jeremy Meinen

Chief Financial Officer

contact@relieftherapeutics.com

DISCLAIMER

This press release contains forward-looking statements, which may be identified by words such as "believe," "assume," "expect," "intend," "may," "could," "will," or similar expressions. These statements are based on current plans and assumptions and are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results, financial condition, performance, or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Such factors include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, market developments, regulatory changes, competitive dynamics, and other risks or changes in circumstances. This communication is provided as of the date hereof, and Relief undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements contained herein as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

SOURCE: Relief Therapeutics Holding SA

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on May 15
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Thursday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

March mining production data (ZAMNG=ECI)

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

South Africa's rand recouped some losses on Wednesday, a day after the country's power utility implemented rolling power cuts. On the stock market, the Top-40 index closed about 0.3% lower.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Stocks struggled for direction on Thursday while the dollar stumbled as the euphoria from market tailwinds earlier in the week fizzled out, with traders looking to U.S. data later in the day for further catalysts.

WALL STREET

The S&P 500 closed slightly higher after flitting between gains and losses during Wednesday's lacklustre session as investors waited for the next batch of economic data after a robust start to the week spurred by soft inflation data and a U.S.-China tariff truce.

GOLD

Gold prices hit an over one-month low on Thursday ahead of a key U.S. data, which is expected to provide cues on the Federal Reserve's monetary policy path, while a thaw in U.S.-China trade tensions also weighed on bullion's appeal.

EMERGING MARKETS

data.title: Thai consumer confidence hits 7-month low in April due to U.S. tariffs
data.description:
Thai consumer confidence dropped for a third straight month in April, hitting its lowest level in seven months due to concerns over U.S. tariffs and a slow domestic economic recovery, a survey showed on Thursday.

The consumer index of the University of the Thai Chamber of Commerce (THCONF=ECI) fell to 55.4 in April from 56.7 in the previous month, the university said in a statement.

"Confidence has steadily declined... due mainly to the trade war", university president Thanavath Phonvichai told a press conference.

"Purchasing power remains stagnant, suggesting confidence is still in a downward trend," he said, adding consumers were hesitant to make purchases, especially on durable goods, homes, vehicles, and travel.

Thailand will face a 36% U.S. tariff if a reduction can't be negotiated before a moratorium expires in July. The U.S. has set a 10% tariff for most nations while the moratorium is in place.

Thailand has sent a trade proposal to Washington as part of its efforts to avoid the tariffs, Finance Minister Pichai Chunhavajira said on Wednesday.

Thanavath said if tariffs remained at 10% for most nations, then it could cut Thailand's economic growth by up to 1 percentage point this year.

Earlier this month, the finance ministry cut its 2025 forecast for growth in Southeast Asia's second-largest economy to 2.1% from 3% due to the impact of U.S. tariffs and a global slowdown.

The economy expanded 2.5% last year, lagging regional peers.

($1 = 33.3900 baht)

data.title: Taruga Minerals Says Rock Chip Sampling at Western Australia Project Confirms Polymetallic Mineralization
data.description:
Taruga Minerals said additional rock chip results from exploration at the Thowagee project in Western Australia confirmed the high-grade lead-silver dominant polymetallic mineralization and extended the zone of outcropping metals, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The sampling demonstrated a result of 12.2% grade of lead, 170 grams per tonne grade of silver, and 0.7% grade of zinc, as well as a result of 13% grade of lead and 102g/t grade of silver.

Two distinct polymetallic mineralised trends have been mapped.

An expanded exploration program, including geochemical soil sampling and further rock chip sampling, is underway at the project, and soil sampling results are expected in the coming weeks.

data.title: Merck KGaA's Q1 Profit, Net Sales Grow
data.description:
Merck KGaA said Thursday its profit and net sales for the first quarter jumped year over year.

Profit after income tax attributable to shareholders for the three months ended March 31 was 735 million euros, compared with 694 million euros a year ago. EPS moved to 1.69 euros from 1.60 euros.

The pharmaceutical company's net sales were 5.28 billion euros, compared with 5.12 billion euros earlier.

For 2025, the company now anticipates net sales between 20.9 billion euros and 22.4 billion euros, reflecting an organic growth of 2% to 6%. Previously, Merck expected net sales of 21.5 billion euros to 22.9 billion euros in 2025, or an organic growth of 3% to 6%.

data.title: Merck KGaA Cuts Outlook Amid Macroeconomic Challenges
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

Germany's Merck KGaA slashed its full-year outlook on the back of challenging macroeconomic conditions and foreign-exchange headwinds.

The life-sciences and electronics company said Thursday that it now expects full-year sales to come in between 20.9 billion and 22.4 billion euros ($23.35 billion-$25.03 billion), previously forecast at between 21.5 billion and 22.9 billion euros. It estimates its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization before one-time items of 5.8 billion euros to 6.4 billion euros, prior seen at between 6.1 billion euros to 6.6 billion euros.

Its Ebitda before one-time items--the company's preferred profitability metric--rose 5.6% to 1.535 billion euros in the first quarter, as sales rose 3.1% to 5.28 billion euros. The company attributed the increase to temporarily reduced research and development expenses in its healthcare segment and strict cost discipline.

Analysts expected pre-items Ebitda at 1.51 billion euros on net sales of 5.28 billion euros, according to consensus estimates provided by the company.

The company's adjusted earnings per share stood at 2.12 euros, compared with 2.06 euros in the prior-year period, below analysts' expectations.

Its profit after tax rose to 738 million euros from 699 million euros in the prior year period.

"We've slightly adjusted our guidance but continue to remain confident that we are well-positioned to achieve sustainable growth for 2025 and beyond," its chief executive officer Belen Garijo said.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: Fibank, Chimimport prop up Sofia bourse indices
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), May 15 (SeeNews) - The majority of the Bulgarian Stock Exchange (BSE) indices advanced on Wednesday, with the blue-chip SOFIX growing 0.28% to 941.47 points, bourse data showed.

SOFIX was bolstered by First Investment Bank, or Fibank, [BUL:FIB], which added 2.88% to 4.64 levs, and diversified group Chimimport [BUL:CHIM], which climbed 2.14% to 0.715 levs.

The two companies also propped up the BGBX40, which tracks the 40 most traded shares on the Sofia bourse, and the BGTR30, in which companies with a free float of at least 10% have equal weight. The biggest gainer in both indices was fertiliser producer Neochim [BUL:NEOH], which increased by 4.42% to 23.60 levs.

Overall, the BGBX40 rose by 0.22% to 177.75 points, while the BGTR30 zoomed 0.25% to 892.12 points.

The BEAMX index, which tracks the ten most highly capitalised growth companies, jumped 1.44% to 90.03 points as biometric identification security systems developer Biodit [BUL:BDT] surged 13.71% to 0.398 levs.

By contrast, the BGREIT, which tracks seven real estate investment trusts, lost 0.09% to 217.39 points due to a 5.33% drop in Sopharma Buildings REIT [BUL:SFB] to 14.20 levs.

Total turnover on the BSE swelled to some 2.46 million levs ($1.4 million/1.3 million euro) on Wednesday from 1.57 million levs on Tuesday.

With a turnover of 383,092 euro from the exchange of 9,892 shares, IoT products maker Shelly Group [BUL:SLYG; ETR:SLYG] was the most liquid stock of the day. It closed 1.83% higher at 39.00 euro.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: KBC Group to Buy Majority Stake in Slovakia's 365.bank
data.description:
Belgian insurer KBC Group agreed to buy a 98.45% stake in 365.bank from Czech Republic-based J&T Finance Group.

The deal, subject to regulatory and anti-trust approvals, values the Slovakian commercial bank at 761 million euros, according to a Thursday announcement.

Closing is expected by the end of 2025.

data.title: RWE posts Q1 profit decline on weak trading, poor winds
data.description:
Key points:
  • Q1 adj EBITDA down 23.5%
  • Keeps 2025 outlook
  • Could buy back more shares if return targets are not met

Germany's largest utility RWE on Thursday said first-quarter core earnings (adjusted EBITDA) fell by nearly a quarter, hurt by a weak commodity trading business as well as poor wind conditions that weighed on the group's offshore business.

First-quarter adjusted EBITDA came in at 1.307 billion euros ($1.46 billion), RWE said, also below the average forecast of 1.321 billion euros in a poll provided by the company.

RWE, which has come under pressure from activist investors to do more share buybacks, still confirmed its 2025 outlook, expecting adjusted EBITDA between 4.55 billion and 5.15 billion euros and a dividend of 1.20 euros per share.

The group earlier this year set more ambitious return targets for new energy projects in light of regulatory hurdles and supply chain risks, also cutting its investment programme by more than a fifth.

It said on Thursday that if these targets, defined as an average rate of return of more than 8.5%, were not met it could adjust its capital allocation and reinvest the savings in additional share buybacks.

RWE is currently buying back up to 1.5 billion euros of its shares in a programme that runs until the second quarter of 2026.

($1 = 0.8933 euros)

data.title: Australia's NRW Holdings tumbles after warning about $73 million impairment
data.description:
Shares of Australia's NRW Holdings fell as much as 24% to a nearly two-year low on Thursday, after it warned that a proposed government legislation regarding Whyalla Ports’ assets could lead to a hit of A$113.3 million ($73 million).

Shares of the infrastructure services provider were down 9.3% at A$2.635, as of 0442 GMT. Stock was the top laggard on the ASX 200 benchmark index , which was up 0.2%.

Earlier this week, the South Australian government drafted legislation that could nullify the lease agreement granted by OneSteel Manufacturing Pty to Whyalla Ports Pty.

The proposed bill allows OneSteel to take over Whyalla Ports' assets without any compensation to the latter.

In February, the NRW's unit Golding Contractors Pty obtained a loan security over the assets and shares of Whyalla Ports, which includes assets under the lease with OneSteel.

"NRW is extremely disappointed and concerned that the proposed and unprecedented intervention by the South Australian government will seriously impair and undermine Golding’s security over Whyalla Ports," NRW Holdings said in a statement.

Golding Contractors has been providing mining services for OneSteel Manufacturing since 2020 and is owed A$113.3 million as per the mining services agreement.

The company is making a provision for the potential impairment in its upcoming full-year results, NRW Holdings said.

($1 = 1.5518 Australian dollars)

data.title: Züblin Immobilien Posts Rise in FY25 Earnings, Rental Income
data.description:
Züblin Immobilien said Thursday that earnings and rental income for fiscal 2025 increased year over year.

Earnings for the 12 months ended March 31 were 8.7 million francs, compared with 1.3 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 2.62 francs from 0.40 franc.

The Swiss real estate company's rental income was 9.4 million francs, compared with 9 million francs a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board will propose an unchanged dividend of 1 franc per share for the period at its 2025 annual general meeting.

data.title: S.Korean won surges as US-Asia FX talks rattle markets
data.description:
Key points:
  • Korean won up 0.59%, broke 1,400 level Wednesday
  • Dollar wavers on uncertainty over trade deals
  • Concerns over dollar depreciation drive currency realignments

The South Korean won extended gains on Thursday, leading a rally in Asian currencies as talks between Seoul's deputy finance minister and U.S. Treasury officials on foreign exchange spurred bets that dollar weakness could be here to stay.

The won rose as much as 1% by 0513 GMT, marking its third consecutive session of gains. On Wednesday, it had surged 1.8% against the U.S. currency to 1,391.40, breaking through the psychologically important 1,400 level.

South Korea's Deputy Finance Minister Choi Ji-young met with Assistant Secretary for International Finance at the U.S. Treasury, Robert Kaproth, to discuss the dollar/won market on May 5.

This sharp movement echoes the unprecedented rally in Taiwan's currency in early May, when the Taiwan dollar recorded a remarkable 6% surge over two days, clocking its largest single-day percentage gain since the 1980s.

On Thursday, the Taiwan dollar continued strengthening, rising as much as 0.5% to 30.136 per U.S. dollar, reaching a one-week high.

The parallels between the won and Taiwan dollar rallies have fuelled market speculation about potential U.S. pressure on its trade surplus partners for currency appreciation. Taiwan's central bank has denied such claims, attributing recent volatility to foreign inflows and market expectations.

However, analysts at Morgan Stanley expect the won's rally to be more limited than Taiwan's currency, citing South Korea's smaller dollar portfolio holdings, weaker export growth and narrowing current account surplus compared with Taiwan.

"In FX strategy, we are neutral on KRW against USD in the near term. We see room for KRW to underperform TWD."

Currency markets found some stability after Bloomberg reported that Washington is not seeking dollar depreciation as part of trade negotiations. Nevertheless, persistent concerns about the U.S. administration potentially favouring a weaker dollar continue to keep investors cautious.

The U.S. dollar index , which measures the currency against six major counterparts, eased 0.2% to 100.9, providing some relief to Asian currencies. The Philippine peso and the Singapore dollar added 0.2%.

The Malaysian ringgit and the Indonesian rupiah largely traded flat.

The currency movements come during a volatile week where initial optimism over the U.S.-China tariff ceasefire has given way to uncertainty about the specifics of various trade agreements.

The agreement in Geneva exceeded market expectations, with the United States and China sharply dropping extra retaliatory tariffs imposed on each other.

Regional equity markets took a breather on Thursday, marking their first subdued session after several days of gains fuelled by the U.S.-China trade pact that had boosted risk appetite for Asian assets.

Shares in Bangkok and Manila fell more than 1%, respectively, while those in Kuala Lumpur each declined 0.5%.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** APEC warns of tariff impact on trade

** Philippines cash remittances up 2.6% y/y in March

** China's c.bank welcomes more panda bond issues

data.title: Lanthanein Resources Encounters Gold Mineralization at Western Australia Project During Drilling
data.description:
Lanthanein Resources said diamond drilling at the Lady Grey project in Western Australia encountered gold mineralization in a modelled electromagnetic conductor plate, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Significant intercepts encountered during the drilling included 4.1 meters at 1.35 grams per tonne grade of gold from 134.66 meters, and 1.27 meters at 0.55 g/t grade of gold from 287.41 meters.

data.title: Siemens confirms growth outlook after Q2 figures beat forecasts
data.description:
Siemens still expects to increase its full-year sales by between 3 and 7% "despite increased uncertainty", the German engineering group said on Thursday, as it reported better-than-expected profit during its second quarter.

The company, whose products include factory software, controllers and trains, said its industrial profit rose 29% to 3.24 billion euros ($3.63 billion) in the three months to the end of March.

The figure, helped by a 315-million-euro gain from the sale of its wiring business to ABB , beat analysts' consensus forecast of 2.75 billion euros.

Sales rose 7% to 19.76 billion euros, ahead of forecasts for 19.22 billion euros, while orders increased 10%.

As a result, Siemens confirmed its outlook for its full-year sales to increase by 3-7% despite seeing "increased uncertainty in the economic environment".

"Our customers continue to rely on our technology, and our global footprint demonstrates our resilience," said Chief Executive Roland Busch in a statement.

In March, Chief Financial Officer Ralf Thomas noted hesitancy among customers due to uncertainties about tariffs, with many delaying investment decisions.

But Siemens, whose results give an indication of the broader industrial economy, said it was seeing an improving situation in most of its businesses.

Although Digital Industries, the company's flagship automation unit, struggled, with a 5% drop in revenue, Siemens said it saw signs of destocking by customers coming to an end.

The weakness was compensated by Smart Infrastructure, which combines hardware and software to manage electricity, heating, cooling, lighting, and data in buildings. It increased sales by 12% while profit jumped 61% helped by the sale of its wire accessories business.

The division is benefiting from sustained demand for electrification, power distribution and the construction of data centres for artificial intelligence.

Mobility also saw revenue and profit rise, buoyed by global investments in rail and transport infrastructure such as electric trains in the United States.

($1 = 0.8931 euros)

data.title: China's Hengrui Pharma Looks to Raise Up to $1.27 Billion in Hong Kong IPO
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals is planning to raise as much as US$1.27 billion via an initial public offering in Hong Kong, joining a wave of Chinese companies seeking to tap the city's market.

The Chinese drugmaker could raise as much as 9.89 billion Hong Kong dollars, equivalent to about US$1.27 billion, making it one of the biggest offerings in the city so far this year.

Hengrui plans to offer 224.52 million shares at an indicative range of HK$41.45 to HK$44.05 each, a Thursday filing showed.

The company, which is already listed in Shanghai, expects to finalize pricing by May 21 and start trading in Hong Kong from May 23. It has secured GIC, Invesco, and Hillhouse as cornerstone investors for the offering. Getting cornerstone investors before an IPO lets companies market their offerings more effectively. That is especially important during periods of heightened volatility.

Hengrui shares in China were last up 1.0% at 53.45 yuan, putting the pharma group's market capitalization at about 34.10 billion yuan, or about US$4.73 billion.

Hong Kong's IPO market has been heating up in recent months, with Chinese companies considering a second listing in the financial hub.

Increased activity from A-share companies has supported an upswing in the city's IPO market this year, Hong Kong Stock Exchange said last month. Funds raised from 17 new listings in the city during the first quarter nearly quadrupled to HK$18.7 billion compared with a year earlier, 3according to HKEX.

Electric-vehicle battery giant Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. will start trading in Hong Kong next week and could raise at least US$4 billion in its IPO.

Founded in 1970, Hengrui focuses on oncology, metabolic and cardiovascular diseases. The company's revenue rose 20% to 7.20 billion yuan in the first quarter of the year.

Citigroup and Morgan Stanley are among the banks advising the pharma company on its IPO.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com
